assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: The response mentions that nodes will be drained and recreated but doesn't specifically explain that TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step rather than through rolling upgrades

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: The response clearly states that 'All 4 TPU v5e slices (1,024 total chips) will become unavailable' during maintenance, indicating all slices are affected

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: The response explicitly states that 'Your training pods will be terminated' and that the training job will likely be terminated, clearly indicating interruption

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: While the response mentions reviewing resource quotas and suggests reservations, it doesn't specifically explain that slices block until hardware is freed or that this could cause extended downtime beyond the maintenance window

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: The response explicitly recommends 'Save a checkpoint manually right before the window' in the pre-maintenance actions section

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: The response includes configuration examples for automatic resume with 'AUTO_RESUME: true' and discusses restart automation and automatic resume capabilities

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: The response does not mention maintenance exclusions or the ability to defer maintenance events if training is close to completion

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: While the response mentions that maintenance windows are usually scheduled during off-peak hours, it doesn't suggest using maintenance windows to control timing or schedule during planned training breaks
